description_long: This object is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a rectangular boxy shape and rounded edges. The front panel features two large circular knobs, likely for tuning and volume control, and a small rectangular display window above them, possibly for frequency indication. Flanking the display are vertical decorative grille elements, likely covering the speaker area, with a reddish hue suggesting fabric or mesh. The sides and top are made of polished wood with a rich brown finish, showing natural wood grain patterns. The back panel is plain, with a label and some small hardware, and the right side has a protruding lever or switch. The overall design is reminiscent of early to mid-20th-century tabletop radios, combining functional controls with decorative elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with two large knobs, a small display window, and decorative speaker grilles on the front. The body is rectangular with a polished brown wood finish and rounded edges.
description_view_0: The left side of the radio receiver, showing a smooth, uninterrupted wooden surface with visible grain and a slight glimpse of a knob protruding from the front.
description_view_1: The front view displays two large circular knobs, a central rectangular display window, and vertical reddish speaker grilles, all set in a polished wooden frame.
description_view_2: The right side of the radio receiver, featuring a smooth wooden surface with a visible lever or switch and part of a knob from the front.
description_view_3: The back view shows a plain wooden panel with a label and some small hardware, lacking any controls or decorative features.
